economic_notes: First Nation has investment in Piapot First Nations Business Trust (modified equity method) which owns Cree Land Mini-Mart Limited Partnership and North Piapot Holdings Limited Partnership. Also holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development) and non-controlling interest in 2544737 Ontario Limited (soft drink and ice manufacturing). Operations impacted by COVID-19 including office closures, travel restrictions, cancellation of events. Received additional ISC funding for COVID-19 related costs but less funding from First Nations Trust due to SIGA casino closures.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). The Nation has a contingent asset related to a QVIDA flood claim against the Government of Canada; a loan of $2,016,399 was taken to fund negotiation costs, secured by insurance. This is noted as an Emphasis of Matter in the auditor's report but opinion is unmodified. Eight entities fully consolidated. Piapot First Nations Business Trust uses modified equity method and owns two limited partnerships. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC confirmed funding ($13,158,128) plus CMHC Subsidy ($760,987) plus FHQ Tribal Council ($628,668) plus CMHC Rent ($244,080) plus CMHC RRAP/Retrofit ($29,400) plus First Nations Trust ($554,707) plus Living Sky CDC ($48,048) plus Living Sky lease dividend ($70,000) = approximately $15,493,018 but net ISC revenue recognized was $16,359,800 (after deferred revenue adjustments); osr calculated as total revenue minus net ISC recognized ($16,359,800) minus CMHC Subsidy ($760,987) minus FHQ Tribal Council ($628,668) minus CMHC Rent ($244,080) minus CMHC RRAP/Retrofit ($29,400). expenses_infrastructure mapped to Community Development segment. annual_debt_service reflects repayment of long-term debt only (excluding capital lease repayments of $109,232).
